pm_research_plan: Problem: Understanding how tuition centers in Penang use their facilities as a strategy to attract and retain students, and how these facility features compare with one another, in order to identify how a new tuition center in Penang can position its own facilities to stand out in the local market. Target Users: Parents of school-going children (primary to secondary level) in Penang who are evaluating tuition centers; and tuition center owners/operators seeking to understand competitive facility positioning. MVP Goal: Identify the facility strategies that existing Penang tuition centers use most effectively and define the minimum set of facility investments a new center must make to be competitive in its chosen location and target segment. Success Metrics: Coverage: Evidence from at least 10 tuition centers across different Penang locations. Comparability: At least 3 distinct facility st ... [compressed 3068 chars] ... acility information? Decision Criteria: Relevance: Only include Penang-based tuition centers with verifiable evidence of facility features. Comparability: Centers must be comparable by location proximity and target student level. Transparency: Each facility feature observation must include source and verification method. Strategic Focus: Prioritize facility features known to influence choice (safety, learning environment, specialization rooms) over secondary factors. Conflict Resolution: If facilities data is missing for some centers, that absence should be reported rather than assumed neutral
